headlines.title: Vance warns that AI should not outrank humans in war
headlines.description: Vice President JD Vance said he worries about how emerging AI will be used in warfare, urging graduating Air Force cadets to not allow technology to supersede their judgment.

headlines.title: Google engineer made $1.2M placing bets on Polymarket using confidential info, prosecutors say
headlines.description: A Google software engineer was accused Wednesday of using confidential company information to make over $1.2 million on Polymarket in the second significant criminal case tied to trades on the prediction market platform.

headlines.title: Illinois Legislature passes historic AI bill that would require third-party safety audits
headlines.description: The measure goes beyond legislation in California and New York regulating America’s largest AI companies. It still needs to be signed by the governor.

headlines.title: Red-hot AI demand pushes more companies into the $1 trillion club
headlines.description: Micron Technologies and SK Hynix joined the trillion-dollar club, which includes Apple, Meta and Tesla.

headlines.title: Gavin Newsom takes a populist turn on AI ahead of a possible 2028 presidential run
headlines.description: AI is becoming a dominant political issue, with many on the right and the left coalescing around concerns about the rapid growth of the industry.

headlines.title: Tech titans mostly silent after Pope Leo’s warning about risks of AI
headlines.description: Pope Leo XIV’s sweeping warning about the dangers of artificial intelligence drew a largely muted response across the American technology world.

headlines.title: Vance calls Pope Leo’s AI warnings ‘profound’
headlines.description: Vance said in an interview with NBC News he is glad the pope tackled AI. He also discussed why X remains off his phone after he deleted it for Lent.

headlines.title: Justice Department launches criminal probe into E. Jean Carroll
headlines.description: According to two sources, the Justice Department has opened a criminal investigation into whether E. Jean Carroll committed perjury during her lawsuits tied to her sexual abuse allegations against President Donald Trump. NBC News' Kelly O'Donnell reports on the investigations against the president's rivals.

headlines.title: Farmers in Iowa are struggling in Trump’s economy, but many say they still support him
headlines.description: 13 farmers across Iowa said they’re struggling economically, but 11 voted for Trump in the past and still largely back him, hoping “he follows through” on his promises.

headlines.title: When drug after drug failed, a teen hoped brain surgery could fix her daily seizures. Insurance denied it.
headlines.description: Several weeks after NBC News reached out to Anthem Blue Cross Blue Shield about the denial, the insurer changed its policy to include coverage of deep brain stimulation for certain children.

headlines.title: NASA’s Jared Isaacman shares moon base plans
headlines.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman talks with NBC News’ Gadi Schwartz about plans for building a base on the moon, rover designs, and what’s next for the Artemis missions.

headlines.title: Pope Leo issues new warning on artificial intelligence
headlines.description: Pope Leo is making history by wading into the AI debate, warning that people need to be a part of developing the new technology, however it unfolds. NBC News’ Anne Thompson has the story.
